title: Boot Process & Filesystem Hierarchy
goal: Inspect a running Linux system's boot configuration, kernel command line, initramfs, and the standard Filesystem Hierarchy to understand how GRUB hands off to the kernel and where FHS directories live.
build: Identify the distro and kernel, inspect GRUB, the kernel cmdline, and initramfs, then tour the FHS and boot-related directories.

steps.title: Identify distribution and architecture
steps.code:
cat /etc/os-release
uname -a
uname -m
uname -r
hostnamectl 2>/dev/null || true
lsb_release -a
steps.explain: Reads the standard distro-ID file and kernel/architecture details.

steps.title: Inspect GRUB configuration
steps.code:
cat /etc/default/grub 2>/dev/null || true
ls /boot 2>/dev/null
find / -name "grub.cfg" 2>/dev/null | head
steps.explain: Views the human-edited GRUB defaults and the generated grub.cfg.

steps.title: Inspect the kernel command line
steps.code:
cat /proc/cmdline
cat /proc/version
ls /proc/sys/kernel/ | head
cat /proc/sys/kernel/hostname
steps.explain: Shows the exact arguments GRUB passed to the running kernel.

steps.title: Explore the initramfs
steps.code:
ls /boot/initrd* 2>/dev/null || echo "no initrd image present"
lsinitramfs /boot/initrd.img-$(uname -r) 2>/dev/null | head -30
steps.explain: Lists the contents of the initramfs cpio archive without extracting it.

steps.title: Tour the Filesystem Hierarchy (FHS)
steps.code:
for d in /bin /boot /dev /etc /home /lib /proc /sbin /sys /tmp /usr /var /run /opt /srv; do
  printf "%-8s -> " "$d"
  ls -ld "$d" 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $1, $NF}'
done
ls /usr
ls /var
file /bin /sbin /lib 2>/dev/null
steps.explain: Walks each top-level FHS directory and reveals the usrmerge symlinks.

steps.title: Walk through key boot-related dirs
steps.code:
ls /etc/systemd/system/ | head
systemctl list-units --type=target --no-pager 2>/dev/null | head
ls /lib/modules/$(uname -r) 2>/dev/null | head || ls /lib/modules | head
cat /proc/mounts | head
steps.explain: Shows systemd targets (successors to runlevels) and the kernel module tree.

test: You can identify the running distro/kernel/arch and explain how GRUB, the kernel cmdline, and initramfs bring up userspace.

title: Kernel Modules & Device Management
obj_title: Summarize Linux device management concepts and tools
goal: List, inspect, load, and unload kernel modules and use the standard hardware-enumeration tools to understand modprobe vs insmod, module dependencies, and reading dmesg.
build: Install discovery tools, inspect and load/unload a module, examine module dependencies, read the kernel ring buffer, and enumerate hardware.

steps.title: Install hardware inspection tools
steps.code:
apt-get update -qq
apt-get install -y pciutils usbutils hwinfo lshw dmidecode kmod >/dev/null
steps.explain: Installs discovery utilities and kmod, which provides the module tools.

steps.title: List loaded modules and inspect one
steps.code:
lsmod | head -20
lsmod | wc -l
MOD=$(lsmod | awk 'NR==2 {print $1}')
modinfo "$MOD" | head -20
steps.explain: Lists currently loaded modules from /proc/modules and shows one module's metadata.

steps.title: Load and unload a module
steps.code:
modprobe dummy 2>&1 || modprobe loop 2>&1 || echo "module load blocked"
lsmod | grep -E "dummy|loop" | head
ip link add dummy0 type dummy 2>/dev/null && ip link show dummy0
ip link del dummy0 2>/dev/null
modprobe -r dummy 2>&1 || true
steps.explain: Loads a module with dependency resolution then removes it with modprobe -r.

steps.title: Module dependencies and depmod
steps.code:
cat /lib/modules/$(uname -r)/modules.dep 2>/dev/null | head -5
depmod -a 2>&1 | head || echo "depmod requires module tree"
modprobe --show-depends loop 2>&1 | head
steps.explain: Shows the modules.dep dependency map that depmod builds and modprobe uses.

steps.title: Read the kernel ring buffer
steps.code:
dmesg | tail -20 2>/dev/null || journalctl -k --no-pager | tail -20
dmesg -T 2>/dev/null | tail -5 || true
steps.explain: Reads kernel messages where module load and hardware probe events appear.

steps.title: Enumerate hardware
steps.code:
lscpu
lsmem 2>/dev/null | head || free -h
lspci | head
lsusb 2>/dev/null | head
lsblk
lshw -short 2>/dev/null | head -20
steps.explain: Uses each ls* tool to read a different kernel interface for hardware discovery.

steps.title: initramfs builders overview
steps.code:
which dracut 2>/dev/null || echo "dracut on RHEL/Fedora/SUSE"
which mkinitramfs && mkinitramfs --help 2>&1 | head
which update-initramfs && update-initramfs --help 2>&1 | head
steps.explain: Contrasts Debian/Ubuntu mkinitramfs with the RHEL-family dracut.

test: You can pick the right ls* tool for a hardware question and explain insmod vs modprobe vs depmod.

title: Storage with LVM, Partitions & Filesystems
obj_title: Manage storage in a Linux system
goal: Build a complete LVM stack on loopback-backed disks, format with ext4/xfs/btrfs, mount with hardening options, persist in fstab, and grow a filesystem online.
build: Create loopback disks, partition, build PVs/VG/LVs, make filesystems, mount with hardening options into fstab, then extend an LV and its filesystem live.

steps.title: Install tooling and create loopback disks
steps.code:
apt-get install -y lvm2 xfsprogs btrfs-progs parted util-linux >/dev/null
mkdir -p /lab/disks && cd /lab/disks
for i in 1 2 3; do
  truncate -s 512M disk${i}.img
  losetup -fP disk${i}.img
done
losetup -a
steps.explain: Creates three sparse image files and attaches them as loopback block devices.

steps.title: Partition the first loopback disk
steps.code:
LOOP1=$(losetup -j /lab/disks/disk1.img | cut -d: -f1)
parted -s "$LOOP1" mklabel gpt
parted -s "$LOOP1" mkpart primary 1MiB 100%
parted -s "$LOOP1" set 1 lvm on
partprobe "$LOOP1"
steps.explain: Writes a GPT label and one LVM-flagged partition spanning the disk.

steps.title: Create PVs, a VG, and LVs
steps.code:
LOOP2=$(losetup -j /lab/disks/disk2.img | cut -d: -f1)
LOOP3=$(losetup -j /lab/disks/disk3.img | cut -d: -f1)
pvcreate "${LOOP1}p1" "$LOOP2" "$LOOP3"
vgcreate labvg "${LOOP1}p1" "$LOOP2" "$LOOP3"
lvcreate -L 200M -n lv_ext4 labvg
lvcreate -L 200M -n lv_xfs  labvg
lvcreate -L 200M -n lv_btrfs labvg
steps.explain: Writes LVM metadata on the devices, pools them into a VG, and carves out LVs.

steps.title: Make filesystems
steps.code:
mkfs.ext4  -F /dev/labvg/lv_ext4
mkfs.xfs   -f /dev/labvg/lv_xfs
mkfs.btrfs -f /dev/labvg/lv_btrfs
blkid /dev/labvg/lv_ext4 /dev/labvg/lv_xfs /dev/labvg/lv_btrfs
steps.explain: Formats each LV and reads back the UUID/TYPE used by fstab.

steps.title: Mount with hardening options and persist in fstab
steps.code:
mkdir -p /mnt/ext4 /mnt/xfs /mnt/btrfs
mount -o noatime,nodev,nosuid,noexec /dev/labvg/lv_ext4  /mnt/ext4
mount -o noatime,nodev,nosuid         /dev/labvg/lv_xfs   /mnt/xfs
mount -o noatime,compress=zstd        /dev/labvg/lv_btrfs /mnt/btrfs
UUID=$(blkid -s UUID -o value /dev/labvg/lv_ext4)
echo "UUID=$UUID  /mnt/ext4  ext4  noatime,nodev,nosuid,noexec  0 2" >> /etc/fstab
df -hT /mnt/ext4 /mnt/xfs /mnt/btrfs
steps.explain: Mounts each filesystem with security options and adds a persistent fstab entry by UUID.

steps.title: Grow a logical volume and its filesystem online
steps.code:
lvextend -L +100M /dev/labvg/lv_ext4
resize2fs /dev/labvg/lv_ext4
lvextend -L +100M /dev/labvg/lv_xfs
xfs_growfs /mnt/xfs
lvresize -L +50M -r /dev/labvg/lv_btrfs
df -hT /mnt/ext4 /mnt/xfs /mnt/btrfs
steps.explain: Extends each LV and grows the filesystem live; lvresize -r does both at once.

test: You can go from raw block device to a mounted, UUID-referenced, online-expandable filesystem; note XFS grows but cannot shrink.

title: Network Configuration
obj_title: Manage network services and configurations on a Linux server
goal: Inspect interfaces, addresses, and routes with ip; walk the name-resolution stack; validate a netplan YAML; and use the standard diagnostic toolbox to read a Linux network stack top-to-bottom.
build: Install network tools, inspect interfaces/routes, walk the resolution stack, run DNS diagnostics, validate netplan, check sockets/connectivity, and capture packets/scan ports.

steps.title: Install tools
steps.code:
apt-get update -qq
apt-get install -y iproute2 iputils-ping dnsutils curl mtr-tiny tcpdump traceroute nmap network-manager netplan.io >/dev/null
steps.explain: Installs the full networking toolbox plus netplan and NetworkManager.

steps.title: Interfaces, addresses, routes
steps.code:
ip -br link
ip -br address
ip route
ip -6 route
cat /proc/net/dev | head
steps.explain: Shows compact interface state/addresses and the IPv4/IPv6 routing tables.

steps.title: Name resolution stack
steps.code:
cat /etc/hosts
cat /etc/resolv.conf
cat /etc/nsswitch.conf | grep -E "^hosts"
getent hosts localhost
getent hosts killercoda.com
steps.explain: Walks the resolution path and tests it via the full NSS stack with getent.

steps.title: DNS diagnostics
steps.code:
dig +short killercoda.com
dig +short MX gmail.com
dig +trace example.com 2>&1 | head -20
nslookup example.com
steps.explain: Queries DNS directly, including a root-down +trace of the resolution path.

steps.title: NetworkManager and netplan syntax
steps.code:
nmcli device status 2>&1 | head
ls /etc/netplan/
cat > /etc/netplan/99-lab.yaml <<'EOF'
network:
  version: 2
  renderer: networkd
  ethernets:
    lo:
      addresses:
        - 127.0.0.2/32
EOF
chmod 600 /etc/netplan/99-lab.yaml
netplan generate
steps.explain: Writes a netplan YAML and validates it with netplan generate without applying.

steps.title: Sockets and connectivity tests
steps.code:
ss -tulnp | head
ss -s
ping -c 3 127.0.0.1
curl -sI https://killercoda.com | head -5
traceroute -n -m 5 1.1.1.1
steps.explain: Lists listening sockets and runs ping/curl/traceroute connectivity checks.

steps.title: Packet capture and port scan
steps.code:
timeout 3 tcpdump -i any -nn -c 5 'icmp or port 53' 2>&1 &
sleep 1
ping -c 2 1.1.1.1 >/dev/null
dig +short example.com >/dev/null
wait
nmap -sT -p 1-1024 127.0.0.1 | head -20
steps.explain: Captures live ICMP/DNS packets with tcpdump and TCP-connect scans localhost with nmap.

test: You can read the resolution path (nsswitch -> hosts/resolv.conf -> DNS) and pick the right diagnostic: ss, dig/getent, tcpdump, nmap.

title: Shell Operations & Text Processing
obj_title: Manage a Linux system using common shell operations
goal: Work with shell environment variables, login vs interactive startup files, every common redirection form, and the classic text-processing pipeline to assemble non-trivial one-liners.
build: Explore env vars and PATH, paths and startup files, all redirection operators, then grep/cut/sort/uniq, sed/awk, tr/xargs, and scripted editors.

steps.title: Environment variables and PATH
steps.code:
echo "USER=$USER  HOME=$HOME  SHELL=$SHELL"
echo "PATH=$PATH"
env | head
export LABVAR="hello linux+"
env | grep LABVAR
unset LABVAR
steps.explain: Shows the exported environment and demonstrates export/unset.

steps.title: Absolute vs relative paths and startup files
steps.code:
mkdir -p /tmp/lab5 && cd /tmp/lab5
pwd
ls ./
ls ../tmp
cat ~/.bashrc 2>/dev/null | head || echo "no .bashrc"
cat /etc/profile | head
steps.explain: Contrasts absolute/relative paths and the login vs interactive startup files.

steps.title: Redirection operators
steps.code:
echo "first line"  > out.txt
echo "second line" >> out.txt
wc -l < out.txt
cat <<EOF >> out.txt
line three
line four
EOF
grep line <<<"line via here-string"
ls /etc | head | tee dirlist.txt | wc -l
steps.explain: Demonstrates truncate/append, stdin redirect, here-doc, here-string, and tee.

steps.title: grep, cut, sort, uniq, wc
steps.code:
cp /etc/passwd .
wc -l passwd
grep -c bash passwd
cut -d: -f1,7 passwd | head
cut -d: -f7 passwd | sort | uniq -c | sort -rn
steps.explain: Builds a login-shell frequency report from /etc/passwd.

steps.title: sed and awk
steps.code:
sed -n '1,5p' passwd
sed 's|/bin/bash|/bin/zsh|' passwd | head -3
awk -F: '$3 >= 1000 {print $1, $3, $7}' passwd
awk -F: 'BEGIN{n=0} /nologin/{n++} END{print n" nologin accounts"}' passwd
steps.explain: Uses sed for stream editing and awk for field-based filtering and counting.

steps.title: head, tail, tr, xargs
steps.code:
head -3 passwd
tail -3 passwd
echo "MIXED case STRING" | tr 'a-z' 'A-Z'
echo "one two three" | tr ' ' '\n'
ls /etc | head -5 | xargs -I{} echo "found: {}"
steps.explain: Slices files, translates characters with tr, and turns stdin into args with xargs.

steps.title: Editors: nano and vim, scripted
steps.code:
apt-get install -y vim nano >/dev/null
cat > script.sh <<'EOF'
#!/bin/bash
echo "hello from $(whoami) on $(hostname)"
EOF
chmod +x script.sh
./script.sh
vim -e -s -c 'normal Goappended line' -c 'wq' script.sh
cat script.sh
steps.explain: Writes and runs a script, then edits it non-interactively with vim Ex commands.

test: You can compose grep/awk/sed/cut/sort/uniq/tr/xargs into ad-hoc pipelines and know which startup file each shell type sources.

title: Backup & Restore
obj_title: Perform backup and restore operations for a Linux server
goal: Create tar/cpio/dd archives with gzip/bzip2/xz compression, image and recover disks with dd and ddrescue, mirror trees with rsync, and verify integrity with sha256sum.
build: Make sample data, archive with tar and cpio, dd a disk image, recover a damaged one with ddrescue, mirror with rsync --delete, then verify checksums and read compressed files in place.

steps.title: Install tooling and create sample data
steps.code:
apt-get install -y tar cpio gzip bzip2 xz-utils rsync gddrescue coreutils >/dev/null
mkdir -p /lab6/src/{etc,logs,docs}
cp -r /etc/hostname /etc/os-release /lab6/src/etc/
for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do
  dd if=/dev/urandom of=/lab6/src/logs/log${i}.bin bs=1K count=20 status=none
done
echo "important document" > /lab6/src/docs/notes.txt
steps.explain: Builds a small fake tree of configs, binary logs, and a text file to archive.

steps.title: tar with gzip, bzip2, and xz
steps.code:
cd /lab6
tar -czvf src.tar.gz  src/ 2>&1 | tail -5
tar -cjvf src.tar.bz2 src/ 2>&1 | tail -2
tar -cJvf src.tar.xz  src/ 2>&1 | tail -2
tar -tzf src.tar.gz | head -5
tar -xzvf src.tar.gz -C restore/ 2>&1 | tail -3
diff -r src restore/src && echo "restore matches source"
steps.explain: Creates gzip/bzip2/xz tarballs, lists one, extracts it, and verifies against the source.

steps.title: cpio (the older archiver)
steps.code:
cd /lab6
find src -depth -print | cpio -ov > src.cpio 2>/dev/null
mkdir cpio-restore && cd cpio-restore
cpio -idv < ../src.cpio 2>&1 | tail -3
cd /lab6
steps.explain: Archives via find|cpio and restores it, the same format used by initramfs.

steps.title: dd a disk image to a loopback file
steps.code:
cd /lab6
truncate -s 64M srcdisk.img
mkfs.ext4 -F srcdisk.img >/dev/null
LOOP=$(losetup -fP --show srcdisk.img)
mount "$LOOP" mnt
echo "payload" > mnt/file.txt
umount mnt; losetup -d "$LOOP"
dd if=srcdisk.img of=backup.img bs=1M status=progress
sha256sum srcdisk.img backup.img
steps.explain: Block-copies a disk image with dd and confirms a bit-for-bit match via sha256sum.

steps.title: ddrescue from a damaged image
steps.code:
cd /lab6
cp backup.img damaged.img
dd if=/dev/zero of=damaged.img bs=1 count=1024 seek=10240 conv=notrunc status=none
ddrescue -d -r3 damaged.img recovered.img recovered.log 2>&1 | tail -5
cat recovered.log | head
steps.explain: Simulates bad sectors then recovers what it can with ddrescue, logging failures.

steps.title: rsync local-to-local with delete
steps.code:
mkdir -p /lab6/mirror
rsync -avz /lab6/src/ /lab6/mirror/ | tail -5
echo "new" > /lab6/src/docs/new.txt
rm /lab6/src/logs/log1.bin
rsync -avz --delete /lab6/src/ /lab6/mirror/ | tail -8
diff -r /lab6/src /lab6/mirror && echo "mirror in sync"
steps.explain: Mirrors a tree and uses --delete to prune files removed from the source.

steps.title: Integrity and reading compressed files
steps.code:
cd /lab6
sha256sum src.tar.gz src.tar.bz2 src.tar.xz > checksums.txt
sha256sum -c checksums.txt
zcat src.tar.gz | tar -t | head -5
gzip -l src.tar.gz
steps.explain: Validates checksums and reads compressed archives without a separate decompress step.

test: You can choose tar/cpio/dd/ddrescue/rsync appropriately and know sha256sum -c and rsync trailing-slash/--delete semantics.

title: Virtualization with QEMU/KVM & libvirt
goal: Install QEMU and libvirt, manipulate virtual disk images with qemu-img, list libvirt domains/networks with virsh, and boot a tiny guest under QEMU to discuss hypervisors, image formats, snapshots, and network modes.
build: Install QEMU/libvirt, create and inspect disk images, convert/resize them, take qcow2 snapshots, list libvirt domains/networks, boot a guest under TCG, and review the three network modes.

steps.title: Install QEMU and libvirt
steps.code:
apt-get install -y qemu-system-x86 qemu-utils libvirt-clients libvirt-daemon-system bridge-utils >/dev/null
ls -l /dev/kvm 2>/dev/null && echo "KVM available" || echo "KVM NOT available - use TCG"
qemu-system-x86_64 --version | head -1
virsh --version
steps.explain: Installs the emulator and management daemon and detects KVM vs TCG.

steps.title: Create and inspect disk images
steps.code:
mkdir -p /lab7 && cd /lab7
qemu-img create -f qcow2 disk.qcow2 1G
qemu-img create -f raw   disk.raw   256M
qemu-img info disk.qcow2
qemu-img info disk.raw
steps.explain: Creates a sparse qcow2 and a flat raw image and inspects their metadata.

steps.title: Convert and resize images
steps.code:
cd /lab7
qemu-img convert -O raw disk.qcow2 disk-from-qcow.raw
qemu-img convert -O qcow2 disk.raw disk-from-raw.qcow2
qemu-img resize disk.qcow2 +512M
qemu-img info disk.qcow2 | grep -E "virtual size|disk size"
steps.explain: Interconverts qcow2/raw and grows the virtual size of the qcow2 image.

steps.title: Snapshots inside a qcow2
steps.code:
cd /lab7
qemu-img snapshot -c clean disk.qcow2
qemu-img snapshot -l disk.qcow2
qemu-img snapshot -c after-config disk.qcow2
qemu-img snapshot -d clean disk.qcow2
qemu-img snapshot -l disk.qcow2
steps.explain: Creates, lists, and deletes internal qcow2 snapshots.

steps.title: libvirt: domains and networks
steps.code:
systemctl start libvirtd 2>/dev/null || service libvirtd start 2>/dev/null || true
virsh list --all
virsh net-list --all
virsh net-dumpxml default 2>/dev/null | head -20
virsh capabilities 2>&1 | head -20
steps.explain: Starts libvirtd and lists defined domains and the default NAT network.

steps.title: Boot a minimal guest under QEMU (TCG fallback)
steps.code:
cd /lab7
ACCEL="tcg"
[ -e /dev/kvm ] && ACCEL="kvm"
echo "Using accel=$ACCEL"
timeout 8 qemu-system-x86_64 \
  -machine accel=$ACCEL \
  -m 128 \
  -nographic \
  -serial mon:stdio \
  -drive file=disk.qcow2,if=virtio,format=qcow2 \
  -boot order=c 2>&1 | tail -20 || true
steps.explain: Boots QEMU headless under TCG (or KVM if available) to prove the emulator works.

steps.title: Discuss network modes (concept)
steps.code:
cat <<'EOF'
Bridged   : guest is a peer on the LAN with its own DHCP IP.
NAT       : guest on virbr0; host masquerades outbound (libvirt default).
Host-only : isolated bridge, no upstream; host<->guest only.
EOF
ip link show type bridge 2>/dev/null
steps.explain: Summarizes bridged, NAT, and host-only guest network modes.

test: You can distinguish QEMU (emulator) from KVM (accelerator), qcow2 from raw, internal vs libvirt snapshots, and the three guest network modes.
